basics.name: Zifei Dong
basics.title: AI Product / Conversational AI Research | Dialogue Evaluation, Companion UX, Multi-Model Strategy
summaries: I am looking for 2026 roles in conversational AI product, AI companion design, model evaluation, and applied research for user-facing LLM systems.

My recent work turns long-horizon Chinese dialogue experience into evaluation cases, product hypotheses, and structured review workflows focused on emotional support, clarification, continuity, and everyday assistant behavior.
basics.metaDescription: Zifei Dong builds Chinese dialogue evaluation, companion assistant strategy, and multi-model behavior research for user-facing AI products.

focus.title: Interaction quality that matters after the first turn
focus.intro: I focus on repeated-use AI systems where correctness alone is not enough. The questions I care about are product questions: what builds trust, when users feel understood, and how model behavior shapes whether a conversation stays useful over time.

themes.title: Implicit user-signal recognition
themes.description: How well a model notices uncertainty, emotional undertones, or confirmation-seeking when the user does not state the need directly.

themes.title: Companion and support stance
themes.description: How assistants choose between comforting, clarifying, challenging, or stepping back, especially in emotionally loaded Chinese dialogue.

themes.title: Everyday assistant trust
themes.description: How search behavior, pacing, closure, and tone affect whether a model feels reliable in normal daily decisions rather than one-off demos.

paragraphs: My research focuses on repeated-use AI systems in Chinese-language settings, especially where interaction quality depends on implicit need recognition, conversational continuity, and companion-like support rather than one-turn correctness alone.

I work in a product-facing way: distilling long-term usage into high-signal cases, building rubrics and error taxonomy, and comparing model behavior in forms that are useful for assistant design, evaluation, and strategy.

experience.title: User signal modeling
experience.label: Interaction Quality
responsibilities: Modeling how users express uncertainty, emotional undertones, and confirmation-seeking without stating them explicitly.
Looking at how assistants detect low-salience signals and translate them into the right next conversational move.

experience.title: Dialogue evaluation design
experience.label: Benchmarking
responsibilities: Designing case buckets, rubrics, and taxonomy for long-horizon dialogue where stance, pacing, and continuity matter.
Turning qualitative interaction observations into structured evaluation assets that teams can reuse.

experience.title: Companion assistant strategy
experience.label: Product Direction
responsibilities: Studying when assistants should comfort, clarify, challenge, or step back in emotionally loaded conversations.
Using those patterns to reason about companion UX, support boundaries, and better response defaults.

experience.title: Multi-model behavior analysis
experience.label: Model Comparison
responsibilities: Comparing GPT, Claude, and Gemini across recurring Chinese everyday tasks rather than isolated prompts.
Tracking stable differences in search use, task framing, tone, and closure to inform assistant strategy.

projects.name: Chinese Dialogue Benchmark Showcase
projects.title: Behavior Benchmark for Chinese Emotional Support and Companion-Like Dialogue
projects.description: A public-facing benchmark showcase distilled from long-horizon real dialogue, built to evaluate how Chinese-language models handle implicit needs, continuity, emotional support, and calibration.
features: Built a benchmark with 16 formal cases, 4 user-state buckets, and 48 baseline results across GPT, Claude, and Gemini
Created a semi-automated distillation workflow from private dialogue logs to patterns, case packs, rubrics, taxonomy, and strategy hypotheses
Turned the research into a static public showcase with core cases, reports, and model-by-model evidence for hiring and product discussion

projects.name: Everyday Assistant Behavior Study
projects.title: Multi-Model Study of Chinese Everyday Assistant Behavior
projects.description: An ongoing product research project on how different frontier models behave in Chinese everyday-use settings where trust, pacing, and closure matter as much as correctness.
features: Compared model behavior across consumer decisions, product evaluation, cooking, life planning, and AI workflow tasks drawn from repeated real use
Analyzed systematic differences in intent reading, search invocation, tone, closure strategy, and how each pattern affects trust and task-completion feel
Converted repeated qualitative observations into a reusable comparison framework for demand understanding, tool use, pacing, tone, and closure strategy

education.degree: B.S. in Computer Science and Statistics
